name: Ranged Attack
description: Ranged Attack is an interaction that creates threat, defense, damage, timing, or tactical pressure during conflict.
design_notes: Ranged Attack should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For combat mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use ranged attack as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect ranged attack to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels powerful, skillful, and able to read danger under pressure.

parameters.name: damage_value
parameters.description: Base amount of damage or threat applied by the mechanic.

parameters.name: startup_time
parameters.description: Delay before the active effect begins.

parameters.name: recovery_time
parameters.description: Delay after use before the actor can fully act again.

edge_cases: Ranged Attack is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Ranged Attack overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Ranged Attack runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Ranged Attack state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Ranged Attack plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Ranged Attack produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Ranged Attack strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Ranged Attack becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Ranged Attack with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Ranged Attack as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Ranged Attack with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Ranged Attack as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
